certification_status_detail: This is PARTIAL calibration evidence. Gate 4 (precompute budget) is closed. Gates 1-3 (pre-calibration, hardware qualification, full timing calibration) are OPEN. This record does NOT constitute class certification. Profile row insertion is BLOCKED until all 7 gates pass. Do not reference this record as evidence that gpu-small-v2 is calibrated.

gate_closure.gate_1_pre_calibration: OPEN — no positive control hardware available
gate_closure.gate_2_hardware_qualification: OPEN — no positive or negative control hardware available
gate_closure.gate_3_full_timing_calibration: OPEN — requires staging server + both devices

errata_reference: See ERRATUM-001-M-calculation.md for full correction. Frozen calibration plan not rewritten.

proposed_profile.target_working_set_description: 50% of 8 GB floor = 4 GB (A matrix: M×N×4)

pre_calibration.hardware_description: DEFERRED — requires 8 GB T2 positive control device (community hardware)
pre_calibration.dimension_selection_notes: M=262,144 derived from: floor(4 GB / (4096 × 4)) = 262,144. Produces 4 GB A matrix (50% of 8 GB floor). K=8 per spec tractability constraint. mix_rounds=1 (gpu-small). N=4096 per Phase 2A consistency.

positive_control.selection_notes: Requires: single 8 GB GPU, T2 tier, floor-level. Candidates: RTX 3070 8GB (Ampere), RTX 4060 Ti 8GB (Ada). Must be single device, not multi-GPU. Community participant hardware.

negative_control.selection_notes: Requires: single 6 GB GPU, T1 tier, boundary-close (highest VRAM in T1). Best candidate: RTX 2060 6GB (Turing). Must be compute-competitive with positive control (same or adjacent gen). Community participant hardware.

separation_margin.admissibility_notes: Working set is 4 GB = 50% of 8 GB positive control. Below 65% drift threshold. If separation insufficient at 50%, increase M by 20% (to 314,573) per Section 5.4 Remedy 1.

precompute_budget.target_issuance_rate_challenges_per_minu: DEPLOYMENT DECISION — see SLA scenarios below
precompute_budget.notes: Memory-limited (7 parallel instances), not CPU-limited (12 available). All realistic early-deployment SLA scenarios pass. Scale phase (I=5/min) is marginal at 1.16× headroom.

admissibility_summary.gate_1_pre_calibration: OPEN — requires actual GPU execution on positive control hardware. The pre-staging screening tool (phase2b-calibrate.sh) does not close this gate. CPU reference-kernel timing is not a substitute for GPU timing.
admissibility_summary.gate_2_hardware_qualification: OPEN — requires actual GPU execution of the reduced working set (reduced_M=196,608) on BOTH devices through the staging challenge protocol. nvidia-smi identity alone is not admissible. compute_slowdown must be derived from GPU medians, not CPU medians.
admissibility_summary.gate_3_full_timing_calibration: OPEN — requires 20 full challenges per device through the staging challenge protocol. Server-measured checkpoint_received_at − stage_issued_at is the authoritative timing. No staging server exists yet.
admissibility_summary.gate_4_precompute_budget: CLOSED (2026-03-18) — CPU reference-kernel precompute measured on Intel Ultra 9 285K. This gate is correctly CPU-based per calibration plan Section 6.
admissibility_summary.gate_6_profile_row_insertion: OPEN — depends on Gate 5 + insertion governance
admissibility_summary.gate_7_post_insertion_validation: OPEN — depends on Gate 6 + production deployment
admissibility_summary.overall: NOT CERTIFIED — 1 of 7 gates closed. Gates 1-3 require both community hardware AND a staging challenge server with actual GPU execution. The pre-staging screening tool is preparatory only.
blockers: Community hardware: one 8 GB T2 single-GPU device + one 6 GB T1 single-GPU device
Staging challenge server: must issue challenges and measure GPU-side timing through the actual protocol
Neither blocker is addressed by the pre-staging screening tool
